items.title: SEO for Developers: Beyond Meta Tags
items.description:
Most developers treat SEO as a checklist of meta tags that the marketing team asks for. We add a title, a description, and maybe some Open Graph tags, then we assume the job is done. But modern SEO is less about keywords and more about engineering performance and accessibility.

items.title: Cloud Networking Fundamentals: How a VPC Actually Works
items.description: TL;DR: A VPC is your own private, isolated network in the cloud. You carve it into public subnets (things that face the internet) and private subnets (databases, app servers), then control traffic with route tables and security groups. Default everything to private.<

items.title: create-next-mui v0.2.0 is here 🚀 — Now with Plugin Support!
items.description:
We've just released v0.2.0 of create-next-mui, making it even faster to scaffold a Next.js + MUI project.

The biggest addition in this release is plugin support. You can now install supported integrations into your project with a single command.

items.title: The Hidden Cost of "Outdated Docs": How to Maintain JSDoc Without Adding Technical Debt
items.description:
Stop forcing developers to choose between shipping features and writing documentation.

Every developer has been there: You join a new project, clone the repo, open the setup guide or look at a function header, and realize... the documentation is lying to you.

items.title: Compose your agent prompts once, compile them to every harness
items.description: If you build LLM agents, you probably write the same prompt more than once. A Markdown config for one tool, a rules file for another, and then the actual [{role, content}] messages your SDK sends. The instructions are the same. items.title: What 400 viral LinkedIn posts taught me: 16 hook formulas, now open-source Claude Code skills
items.description: I spent a month doing something slightly unhinged: I pulled 400 above-average LinkedIn posts across 10 verticals, ran them through a fleet of analysis agents, and reverse-engineered why their first lines worked. Then I turned the findings into open-source skills for Claude Code and Codex.

items.title: Why Your AI Assistant Forgets Everything Between Conversations
items.description:

items.title: translateZ()
items.description: The translateZ() function moves an element closer to or farther from the user. translateZ() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.

items.title: translateY()
items.description: The translateY() function shifts an element vertically by the specified amount. translateY() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.

items.title: translateX()
items.description: The translateX() function shifts an element horizontally by the specified amount. translateX() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.

items.title: translate()
items.description: The CSS translate() function shifts an element from its default position on a 2-dimensional plane translate() originally handwritten and published with love on CSS-Tricks. You should really get the newsletter as well.
